title: The big data challenges of connectomics
abstract: Connectomics began in the 1970s with the study of the small nervous system of a worm and has recently garnered general interest thanks to technical and computational advances that automate the collection of electron-microscopy data. This perspective discusses the enormous data challenges posed by connectomics at the petabyte scale.
notes: Key paper on the computational/storage challenges of connectomics. Co-authored with Shavit.
